title: Level 129
wikitext:
{{Rewrite|date= May 20th, 2026|The author has likely never heard of “show, don't tell,” since they had to point out several times the spooky atmosphere the level is engulfed in. The reason why the information is behind a password is unclear, since — for me personally — I'd rather appreciate being told what going near a golden orb does, and it feels like a stupid decision on M.E.G. 's part. The log also doesn't take itself seriously, especially for something that is meant to be serious. Nonetheless that doesn't justify it just reexplaining the level again, but in a “cringier” way. Also, I wouldn't call people who've been dragged into cult entities. Isnt that a bit insensitive? Also, what is the Murphy Headland? I've talked a lot about the phenomenon, so it's time for the level's description. The mechanic of instant healing is interesting, but very unclear. We don't know this short period, and being advised to go to level 128 seems bad, considering that the wanderer had likely decayed by then. How are there even members of the Saudade if they all would've decayed? - [[User:Papashfh|Avian]]. Extra notes: A rewrite does not save a page from deletion. There was no rewrite template, you can always rewrite in a sandbox. - Small.webp Forgive my bluntness, but this is just a copy of Level 128, and too short—47.}} {{Rewrite in Progress |reason=The author has likely never heard of “show, don't tell,” since they had to point out several times the spooky atmosphere the level is engulfed in. The reason why the information is behind a password is unclear, since — for me personally — I'd rather appreciate being told what going near a golden orb does, and it feels like a stupid decision on M.E.G. 's part. The log also doesn't take itself seriously, especially for something that is meant to be serious. Nonetheless that doesn't justify it just reexplaining the level again, but in a “cringier” way. Also, I wouldn't call people who've been dragged into cult entities. Isnt that a bit insensitive? Also, what is the Murphy Headland? I've talked a lot about the phenomenon, so it's time for the level's description. The mechanic of instant healing is interesting, but very unclear. We don't know this short period, and being advised to go to level 128 seems bad, considering that the wanderer had likely decayed by then. How are there even members of the Saudade if they all would've decayed?

 {{CSS|content=.page__right-rail { background-color: rgba(var(--theme-page-background-color--secondary--rgb), 0) !important; } .page__right-rail .rail-module { background-color: transparent !important; } .page__main { background-color: rgba(var(--theme-page-background-color--secondary--rgb), 0) !important; } .page__main .main-module { background-color: transparent !important; } .page__main .main-module { background-color: transparent !important; } :root { --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: rgba(var(--theme-page-background-color--rgb), 1); text-shadow:1px 1px 0 #000, 1px -1px 0 #000, -1px -1px 0 #000, -1px 1px 0 #000; } .theme-fandomdesktop-light { --theme-page-text-color: #fff;} }} {{Background|file=Level KOPPA.png}}__NOTOC__

Class 3
 *Partially Safe [6.2/10 on the Morrison Safety Scale] *Partially Stable [6.31/10 on the Morrison Stability Scale] *Environmental Hazards [6.1/10 on the Morrison Environmental Safety Scale] *Mental Hazards [7.42/10 on the Morrison Mental Safety Scale]
'''Level 129''' is the 130th level of the Backrooms. It was discovered in an unknown and long-ago time period before being rediscovered in 2024. The level is partially merged with [[Level 128]]. ==Description== '''Level 129''' can be divided into two zones: the "Party Zone" and the "Voided Zone". ===Party Zone=== {{Blurimage|file=Level KOPPA.png|caption=The most popular image of ''Level 129'', depicting a darkened party room.}} '''The Party Zone''' is an endless abandoned party place consisting of small party rooms. Each room seen to vary in size; their areas can range from 5m² to 50m². The level occasionally shifts and alters without one expecting or noticing. Most of the interior in this level are painted in orange and yellow, giving off energetic vibes. The lighting system in this area is dysfunctional, as lights tend to flicker at random durations. In the party rooms, multi-colored birthday banners, discolored advertising and propaganda posters, and yellow or orange helium balloons can be found scattered around, arousing an eerie atmosphere. Plastic tables and chairs are organised in the middle of the rooms, holding birthday cakes and hats, paper cups of beverages, cutlery, and [[Object 1|Almond Water]], [[Object 12|Liquid Pain]] and [[Object 44|Cashew Water]]. When one ventures deep enough into this area, the level may become more and more broken apart, where there are holes of different sizes forming on walls and floors, leading to ''The Voided Zone''. Some hallways steeply curve up or down into small rooms with red orbs; going near these red orbs will cause one to instantly die for reasons unknown. Some of these hallways lead into completely different areas when one goes into them multiple times; for example, going through a hallway one time leads to a party room, while going through the same hallway the second time leads to ''the Voided Zone''. The level has some mild medicinal properties upon entering, including healing moderately-sized and smaller wounds, as well as curing minor infections when one stays in it, but this is only for a short amount of time. Afterwards, the wanderer's body will slowly decay and the wanderer will be compelled into going near a glowing golden or red orb. It is advised to leave the level and return to [[Level 128]] instead of moving to a glowing golden or red orb. As mentioned before, the red orbs will cause wanderers to instantly die for unknown reasons upon them getting near it, although golden orbs will cause a hypnotic effect transforming one's mental state into something unrecognizable. ===Voided Zone=== {{Blurimage|file=Balloon in the void.webp|caption=A helium balloon in the Voided Area viewed from [[Level 128]]. Balloons and other items commonly float away into the no-gravity void.}} '''The Voided Zone''' consists of a large zone with no-gravity voided spaces. The Voided Zone is a common crossing point to and from neighboring [[Level 128]], with Murphy Headland being the closest point found. It is unknown what happens if one descends or ascends far from the Voided Zone; however, people theorize that they enter [[the Void]] or disappear. [[Level 128#The Correlation Theory|The Correlation Theory]] states that the Voided Zone was created from significant sections breaking apart from ''Level 129'' and merging with [[Level 128]]. Sometimes, one can even see white orbs in [[Level 128#The Ruined Party|The Ruined Party]] from ''Level 129'', but the Party Hard Effect cannot affect people in ''Level 129'' unless they are near the crossing point of the Voided Zone, and even then, the effect will barely affect any wanderers. These "white orbs" are dismissed as hallucinations, with some of these being white paper balls hanging from the ceiling or being stuck to the ceiling by glue. In antiquity, ''Level 129'' was a highly coveted and sought after place similar to present-day [[Level 128]], however, after the separation of the section that once covered the Voided Area, the level lost this pulling essence. ----
This is a top-secret document which is restricted by the [[M.E.G.]] for safety reasons. If you are to proceed, please insert your username and password.
{{Collapse|initial=Insert Username|num=2|final=-{{USERNAME}}-
|body=
{{Collapse|num=3|initial=Insert Password|final=צ‎ק
|body= ==Addenda== The following is an ancient transcript from an unknown yet long time ago and other addenda.
This is the greatest place thee will ever go to. A Saudade has sent this heaven down to us, we are eternally grateful for thy gift. We are being greedy, A Saudade wants to destroy our gift. We are sorry, let us enjoy the gift, A Saudade, please.
''''''
A Saudade, Portuguese for "The Longing", is a phenomenon which happens when one goes near a golden orangish-yellow colored orb in the center of '''Level 129'''. It is said that people will act unusually after interacting with the orb, worship and obsess over the orb, calling this "appreciation and work for salvation". These people will wear "costumes" made from scrap in '''Level 129''' and attack and indoctrinate other people into worshipping the orb, often attempting to murder people that refuse to "embrace salvation". A Saudade is incurable once a wanderer gets indoctrinated by it, so firearms and other weapons are recommended as people subject to A Saudade can still be vulnerable to any hazards that normal people are vulnerable to. This is an incident that has caused the accidental discovery of '''Level 129''' and the eventual disappearance of an anonymous person who will be dubbed Researcher M for privacy reasons, who had aberrant behavior after the expedition log.

{{Underline|ff9f3e}}
'''Base''': Alright, Researcher ██████████, your job is to retrieve the orb thingy from [[Level 128]]. If ya don't done do it quick, then you're out of this group.
'''Researcher M''': Alright.
[[THREE MINUTES LATER]]
'''Researcher M''': Sir, I found a missing piece in the level, or an empty void I mean. Is this normal?
'''Base''': A void? Can you describe your surroundings?
'''Researcher M''': Well, I'm in The Ruined Party right now, but half of the room is missing, I don't know how to explain it. Hold on, I saw something...
'''Base''': Hello?
[[UNCLEAR SPEECH AND MUMBLING]]
'''Researcher M''': I reached the other side of the void, this seems to be a new area... this looks different...
'''Base''': What's going on? Where are you?
'''Researcher M''': I don't know either, but we should be able to find the orb, whatever ya call it, somewhere here...
[[A FEW HOURS LATER]]
'''Researcher M''': Gosh, this place is just made of voids and parties! I can't find this stupid orb!
'''Base''': You better find it right now, or you're not a part of us anymore, hehe...
'''Researcher M''': I'm trying!
[[FIFTEEN MINUTES LATER]]
'''Researcher M''': Fina-fucking-''ly''! I found that god damn orb. It's not a damn paper or an illusion, right?
'''Base''': Oh yeah, it isn't. Now return it to base.
'''Researcher M''': Gosh no! I'm staying here. Forever.
'''Base''': But why?
'''Researcher M''': I'm not gonna disrespect A Saudade like that you fucking idiot. Now leave me alone so I can get more people to appreciate him and work for salvation.
'''Base''': Since when were you able to speak Portuguese? '''Researcher M''': Gosh, just let me long for the party for God's sake.
{{Underline|ff9f3e}}
Afterwards, Researcher M was found acting and behaving strangely, seemingly doing what he calls "appreciation and work for salvation". It is unknown why, although it is likely that interactions with the orb are to do with this as more people seem to act in more deviant ways after going near the orb.}}}} ==Entities== The most common entities are people affected by A Saudade, [[Entity 3|Smilers]] and [[Sanguine Festivus]]. No other entity has been recorded to be in '''Level 129'''. ==Colonies & Outposts== Only a few colonies and outposts are known to exist. ===Base Q=== *Located in the Murphy Headland; *Friendly; *Has around 35 members; *Helps wanderers traverse through '''Level 129'''; *Open to trading. ===Outpost K=== *Gathers resources from [[Level 128]] and other levels, as well as '''Level 129'''; *Located in the Robinson Headland, the second closest point to [[Level 128]]; *Friendly; *Has around 10 to 20 members; *Subset of the [[M.E.G.]]; *Open to trading. ==Entrances & Exits== ===Entrances=== *The Voided Area serves as a crossing point between both '''Level 129''' and [[Level 128]]. *Doorways with the number "129" lead one here; these doorways are common in [[Level 128]], [[Level 139]], [[Level 4]], et cetera. *Noclipping into an dark-orange birthday cake in any level brings one here. ===Exits=== *The Voided Area serves as a crossing point between both '''Level 129''' and [[Level 128]]. *Doors with a neon exit sign above lead one to [[Level 128]]. *Voids in the level lead one to [[the Void]]. *Certain parts of the level may lead to [[Level Fun]]. {{Collapse|num=1|initial='''Open Author Information'''|final='''Close Author Information'''|body= '''Authors''': [[User:ThatOneMaldivian|ThatOneMaldivian]] and [[User:Brightened Star|Brightened Star]].
'''Rewriters''': Also [[User:ThatOneMaldivian|ThatOneMaldivian]] and [[User:Brightened_Star|Brightened Star]].
'''Music''': [[User:Accountus|Accountus]].
'''Images (Top to Bottom)''': *First image is from WordPress by blogger [https://cassandrafaith.wordpress.com/2014/02/22/21st-meow-party-its-a-black-and-yellow-affair/ Cassandra Faith], edited by [[User:ThatOneMaldivian|ThatOneMaldivian]]. *Second image is a still frame from [https://www.shutterstock.com/video/clip-1007275225-balloon-pop--glitter-explosion-slow-motion this Shutterstock video]. {{User:Brightened Star/Credits|code=|initial=Click for more pages by both authors|final=Click to hide pages by both authors|codeend=|L=|O=
[[Object_27|Mixologist's Delight|'''Object 27: Mixologist's Delight''']]
